skill.name: skill-developer
skill.description: Guides developers through creating, configuring, testing, and troubleshooting Claude Code skills, triggers, guardrails, and hook-based activation.
skill.summary: Build and troubleshoot Claude Code skills with trigger, hook, and rule guidance.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-cryptography patterns, but review shows the skill is Markdown documentation with hook-testing examples rather than bundled executable code. No prompt-injection text, malicious endpoint, credential exfiltration, or cryptographic implementation was found. Publish with a medium warning because the guidance teaches local hook execution and guardrail bypass controls.

medium_findings.title: Local Hook Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The static command-execution findings are mostly fenced documentation examples. They show users how to run local hook scripts with npx tsx and shell wrappers, which is legitimate for this skill but can execute project-controlled code if copied into an untrusted repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes shell and npx hook execution. No bundled executable implementation or malicious command target was found, so this is a contextual risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Guardrail Bypass Controls Documented
medium_findings.description: The skill documents skip markers, environment variables, and session-state deletion that can disable or bypass guardrail checks. These controls are presented as troubleshooting and emergency mechanisms, but users should apply them sparingly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The bypass mechanisms are clearly documented and can weaken enforcement. The surrounding text frames them as user-controlled operational tools, not as hidden or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: External Regex Tester Links Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded network findings point to regex101.com links used for manual pattern testing. I found no evidence that the skill makes network requests or sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in explanatory Markdown text about testing regular expressions. There is no executable network client or credential flow in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections correspond to Markdown tables, examples, or ordinary documentation text. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, encryption code, signing code, or password hashing implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Sampled high-severity lines are documentation structures or regex examples, not cryptographic APIs. A keyword search found no cryptographic implementation terms in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Creating reliable AI skills requires clear triggers, safe hooks, and tested guardrails. This skill provides structured guidance for building and debugging Claude Code skill workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains Claude Code skill structure and YAML frontmatter requirements.
Shows how to configure keyword, intent, file path, and content triggers.
Documents UserPromptSubmit and PreToolUse hook behavior for skill activation.
Provides patterns for guardrail and domain skill rules.
Includes troubleshooting steps for trigger failures and hook execution issues.
limitations: It is documentation and does not create files by itself.
It assumes a Claude Code project with local hook infrastructure.
Examples may require adaptation for each repository layout.
It does not verify third-party hook scripts for safety.

use_cases.title: Create a New Project Skill
use_cases.description: Design a focused skill with clear triggers, frontmatter, and reference files.

use_cases.title: Tune Skill Activation Rules
use_cases.description: Improve keyword, intent, path, and content patterns to reduce false matches.

use_cases.title: Debug Hook-Based Guardrails
use_cases.description: Trace why a skill suggestion or blocking rule did not run as expected.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Claude Code skill for this topic. Include triggers, purpose, structure, and testing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Trigger Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these skill trigger goals. Suggest keyword, intent, file path, and content patterns with false-positive risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Guardrail Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a guardrail skill plan for this risky workflow. Include enforcement level, block message, skip controls, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Skill System
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Claude Code skill setup. Identify weak triggers, hook issues, bypass risks, and documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for database query verification.
output_examples.output: A skill plan with trigger phrases, guardrail behavior, required schema checks, and manual test scenarios.

output_examples.input: My skill does not activate for frontend work.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering keywords, intent patterns, file paths, hook registration, and session state.
A risk review explaining skip markers, environment overrides, session tracking, and safer usage boundaries.

best_practices: Keep the main skill file concise and move detailed references into linked files.
Test triggers with realistic prompts and repository paths before relying on them.
Use bypass controls only for documented exceptions and temporary recovery.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad trigger words that activate skills for unrelated requests.
Do not copy hook commands from an untrusted repository without review.
Do not make guardrails easy to disable without a clear operational reason.

faq.question: Does this skill write Claude Code skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, examples, and troubleshooting steps for creating skills manually.

faq.question: Can it help with both domain and guardrail skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains advisory domain skills and blocking guardrail skills.

faq.question: Does it require local hook scripts?
faq.answer: Some examples assume local hook scripts for activation and enforcement testing.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The files are documentation, but they teach local command execution and guardrail bypass controls.

faq.question: Does it include network code?
faq.answer: No evidence of network code was found. The only URLs are documentation links for regex testing.

faq.question: How should teams use this skill safely?
faq.answer: Review hook scripts, test triggers in a controlled repository, and limit bypass controls.
